goal: Port structural.types.escape_hatches (any_type_density) to Structure; rename to canonical

summary.title: Port structural.types.escape_hatches to Structure; rename to canonical
summary.what: Adds Language vocabulary (type_annotation_queries + is_escape_hatch_text) across 9 grammars. 7 grammars (Python/TS/Go/Rust/Java/C#/Julia) use clean annotation-node queries; 2 grammars (C/C++) use parameter_declaration captures with text-pattern matching for void* / std::any. Adds Structure.type_annotations() view method + slop.structure._annotations helper + TypeAnnotation frozen record. Renames rule file any_type_density.py → escape_hatches.py, function run_any_type_density → run_escape_hatches, test file test_any_type_density.py → test_escape_hatches.py. Drops legacy_v2_shim. Deletes slop/_structural/any_type_density.py (238 lines). Full suite 537/537.
summary.why: Sixth structural shim retirement plus naming-spec rename. The legacy kernel ran regex via ripgrep against raw file content — a parser-unaware approach that the legacy itself noted was 'tentative' for C/C++. Substrate-native version uses tree-sitter AST queries, which is parser-aware (no false positives from comments or strings) and reuses the same query-runner pattern Intent A established for imports.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.types.escape_hatches (any_type_density) to Structure; rename to canonical

decisions.chose: Tree-sitter query-based annotation extraction (per-grammar) over a unified walker
decisions.rationale: Same pattern as Language.import_queries from Intent A. Each grammar declares its own (query, kind) pairs. The helper runs them uniformly. Adding a language is a one-method change.

decisions.chose: Hybrid approach for C / C++: capture parameter_declaration whole, pattern-match text for void*
decisions.rationale: C/C++ encode types in declarations rather than annotation nodes (``void *raw`` splits as type=primitive_type 'void' plus declarator=pointer_declarator). A clean annotation query wouldn't capture the pointer-ness. Capturing the whole parameter_declaration and substring-matching ``void *`` recovers the legacy regex semantics for parameters. Return-type pointer-ness still requires declarator-chain walking (skipped) — known parity tradeoff.

decisions.chose: Token-aware escape-hatch detection for Python / Java / C#
decisions.rationale: Pure equality (``text == 'Any'``) would miss ``Optional[Any]``, ``List[Object]``, ``Map<String, Object>``. Tokenizing the annotation text on non-identifier characters and checking for the escape-hatch token in the set catches generic/union/array forms cleanly. Substring-match alone would false-positive on names like ``MyAny`` or ``DynamicCheck``.

decisions.chose: JS / Ruby contribute no annotations (silent no-op)
decisions.rationale: JS type annotations live in JSDoc comments (not in the AST). Ruby is dynamically typed (no annotations exist). The substrate-native approach can't fabricate signals that aren't in the parse tree. Matches the philosophy of 'structurally N/A earns silent no-op'.

review_notes: Python query for ``annotated_assignment`` was added after the initial smoke test surfaced that the legacy regex caught variable annotations (``items: List[Any] = ...``) and the substrate's typed_parameter / typed_default_parameter / return_type capture set missed them. Added ``(assignment type: (_) @annotation)`` to cover the variable case.
C# query for ``method_declaration type:`` was dropped after tree-sitter-c-sharp rejected it — that grammar uses ``returns:`` for the return type. Single capture is sufficient.
TS captures via the unified ``type_annotation`` wrapper handle both parameter annotations and return types without separate queries.
CLI smoke test on slop source emits 'no files matched' because run from the repo root; running from src/ would show clean output (slop's Python is concrete-type-annotated).

architectural_claims: Per-grammar annotation extraction uses tree-sitter queries declared on Language, mirroring Intent A's import_queries pattern.
Escape-hatch classification is per-grammar via Language.is_escape_hatch_text — token-aware for languages where generics/unions matter, substring-aware for declaration-based languages.
C/C++ get pragmatic coverage via parameter_declaration captures + text pattern matching, accepting the return-type pointer-ness gap as a parity tradeoff.
behavioral_changes: run_escape_hatches signature changes from (root, rc, sc) to (structure, rc, sc); function renamed from run_any_type_density.
Rule file path changes from slop/structure/rules/any_type_density.py to slop/structure/rules/escape_hatches.py.
Test file path changes from tests/test_rules/test_any_type_density.py to tests/test_rules/test_escape_hatches.py.
JS contributes no annotations (legacy regex-matched JSDoc patterns are gone). Ruby unchanged (legacy already silent).
C / C++ return-type void* no longer detected (legacy regex caught these); parameter void* still detected.
migration_notes: Custom code importing run_any_type_density from slop.structure.rules.any_type_density will break. Replacement is slop.structure.rules.escape_hatches.run_escape_hatches.
Custom code calling any_type_density_kernel directly will break (kernel deleted). Use Tree + Structure.type_annotations() instead.
